description: Free from dirt, marks, or unwanted matter; having been washed or purified. 'Hiso' names the quality of cleanliness—surfaces without soil, water without pollution, states of being without contamination.
sound_symbolism: The word clears and refreshes. 'Hi' begins with open breath, air clean. Then 'so' sweeps smoothly, dirt removed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as adjective: 'hiso phialu' (clean water). As verb: 'hiso' (to clean). Related to 'shiloa' (pure) but more practical—about physical cleanliness rather than essential purity.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Clean water, clean air, clean soil—the foundations of healthy ecosystems and communities.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Clean surroundings support clean mind—environment and awareness influence each other.
